Jennifer L. Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Robert Gottschall

RULING:
DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Mr. Cooks' deposition testimony and declaration, Mr. McLean's deposition testimony, Mr. Pantaleoni's deposition testimony and Dr. Bruch's declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Gottschall was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant and whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing his asbestos-related disease. Defendant's objections to Mr. Cooks' declaration are overruled, except to the extent that his declaration references at least ten occasions of working with new Crane Co. valves, which are sustained. Defendant's objections to Mr. McLean's deposition testimony, Mr. Pantaleoni's deposition testimony and Dr. Bruch's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tamara Willat, CSR #4609 = (503/TLJ)

RULING:
Defendant John Crane Inc.'s demurrer
Counsel submitted to Court's tentative ruling, and tentative ruling was adopted: Defendant John Crane Inc.'s demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. It is sustained on the grounds that plaintiffs failed to identify the product associated with defendant as required by Bockrath. Plaintiffs are granted 10 days leave to amend. The demurrer is overruled as to defendant's Boeken argument that there is no difference between the survival and wrongful death negligence and strict liability causes of action. Boeken is inapplicable. C.C.P. 377.20 and 377.60 lay out the survival and wrongful death statutes. 377.60 creates a new cause of action in favor of the decedent's heirs. (Horwich v. Superior Court (1999) 21 Cal.4th 272, 283). Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
